sentences.text: The RVA20 profiles are intended to be used for 64-bit application processors running rich OS stacks.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles

sentences.text: Only user-mode (RVA20U64) and supervisor-mode (RVA20S64) profiles are specified in this family.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles

sentences.text: NOTE: There is no machine-mode profile currently defined for application processor families. A machine-mode profile for application processors would only be used in specifying platforms for portable machine-mode software.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles

sentences.text: Given the relatively low volume of portable M-mode software in this domain, the wide variety of potential M-mode code, and the very specific needs of each type of M-mode software, we are not specifying individual M-mode ISA requirements in the A-family profiles.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles

sentences.text: NOTE: Only XLEN=64 application processor profiles are currently defined.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles

sentences.text: It would be possible to also define very similar XLEN=32 variants.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles

sentences.text: The RVA20U64 profile specifies the ISA features available to user-mode execution environments in 64-bit applications processors.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile

sentences.text: This is the most important profile within the application processor family in terms of the amount of software that targets this profile.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile

sentences.text: RVA20U64 has one optional extension (Zihpm).
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile

sentences.text: RV64I is the mandatory base ISA for RVA20U64, and is little-endian.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: As per the unprivileged architecture specification, the `ecall` instruction causes a requested trap to the execution environment.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: The `fence.tso` instruction is mandatory.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: NOTE: The `fence.tso` instruction was incorrectly described as optional in the 2019 ratified specifications.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: Later versions of correctly indicate that the instruction is mandatory.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: extlink:m[] Integer multiplication and division. - extlink:a[] Atomic instructions. - Single-precision floating-point instructions. - Double-precision floating-point instructions. - extlink:c[] Compressed Instructions. - extlink:zicsr[] CSR instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: These are implied by presence of Zicntr or F. - Basic counters.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must support instruction fetch, and any instruction fetches of naturally aligned power-of-2 sizes up to min(ILEN,XLEN) (i.e., 32 bits for RVA20) are atomic.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The fetch atomicity requirement facilitates runtime patching of aligned instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must support RsrvEventual.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must support AMOArithmetic.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Reservation sets must be contiguous, naturally aligned, and at most 128 bytes in size.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The minimum reservation set size is effectively determined by the size of atomic accesses in the A extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Misaligned loads and stores to main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must be supported.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: This requires misaligned support for all regular load and store instructions (including scalar and vector) but not AMOs or other specialized forms of memory access.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Even though mandated, misaligned loads and stores might execute extremely slowly.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Standard software distributions should assume their existence only for correctness, not for performance.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Hardware performance counters.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Hardware performance counters are a supported option in RVA20.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: The number of counters is platform-specific.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The rationale to not make Q an optional extension is that quad-precision floating-point is unlikely to be implemented in hardware, and so we do not require or expect A-profile software to expend effort optimizing use of Q instructions in case they are present.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Zifencei is not classed as a supported option in the user-mode profile because it is not sufficient by itself to produce the desired effect in a multiprogrammed multiprocessor environment without OS support, and so the instruction cache flush should always be performed using an OS call rather than using the `fence.i` instruction. `fence.i` semantics can be expensive to implement for some hardware memory hierarchy designs, and so alternative non-standard instruction-cache coherence mechanisms can be used behind the OS abstraction. A separate extension is being developed for more general and efficient instruction cache coherence.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The execution environment must provide a means to synchronize writes to instruction memory with instruction fetches, the implementation of which likely relies on the Zifencei extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: For example, RISC-V Linux supplies the `riscvflushicache` system call and a corresponding vDSO call.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Recommendations are not strictly mandated but are included to guide implementers making design choices.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Recommendations

sentences.text: Implementations are strongly recommended to raise illegal-instruction exceptions on attempts to execute unimplemented opcodes.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20U64 Profile > RVA20U64 Recommendations

sentences.text: The RVA20S64 profile specifies the ISA features available to a supervisor-mode execution environment in 64-bit applications processors. RVA20S64 is based on privileged architecture version 1.11.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile

sentences.text: RVA20S64 has one unprivileged option (Zihpm) and one privileged option (Sv48).
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile

sentences.text: RV64I is the mandatory base ISA for RVA20S64, and is little-endian.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: The `ecall` instruction operates as per the unprivileged architecture specification.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: An `ecall` in user mode causes a contained trap to supervisor mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: An `ecall` in supervisor mode causes a requested trap to the execution environment.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: The following unprivileged extensions are mandatory:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The RVA20S64 mandatory unprivileged extensions include all the mandatory unprivileged extensions in RVA20U64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Zifencei is mandated as it is the only standard way to support instruction-cache coherence in RVA20 application processors. A new instruction-cache coherence mechanism is under development which might be added as an option in the future.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The following privileged extensions are mandatory:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: *Ss1p11* Privileged Architecture version 1.11.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The `satp` mode Bare must be supported.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Page-Based 39-bit Virtual-Memory System.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Page-fault exceptions are raised when a page is accessed when A bit is clear, or written when D bit is clear.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must support hardware page-table reads.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: `stvec.MODE` must be capable of holding the value 0 (Direct).
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: When `stvec.MODE=Direct`, `stvec.BASE` must be capable of holding any valid four-byte-aligned address.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: `stval` must be written with the faulting virtual address for load, store, and instruction page-fault, access-fault, and misaligned exceptions, and for breakpoint exceptions that are defined to write an address to stval, other than those caused by execution of the `EBREAK` or `C.EBREAK` instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: For virtual-instruction and illegal-instruction exceptions, `stval` must be written with the faulting instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: RVA20S64 has one unprivileged option.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Hardware performance counters.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The number of counters is platform-specific.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: RVA20S64 has the following privileged options:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Page-Based 48-bit Virtual-Memory System.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: `sstatus.UXL` must be capable of holding the value 2 (i.e., UXLEN=64 must be supported).
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA20 Profiles > RVA20S64 Profile > RVA20S64 Optional Extensions
